evals.prompt: We've finished the architecture session for GlobalBank. I have s2-02, s2-03 and s2-04 ready and want to estimate the delivery effort. Where do we start?
evals.expected_output: Session opening for Phase A: confirms the Session 2 inputs, shows the four-phase TEP structure (assumptions, task inventory, sizing, estimation roll-up), confirms the T-shirt conversion table, and asks Phase 1 assumption questions. Does NOT produce numbers before building the task inventory.

assertions.name: phase-a-session-header-present
assertions.description: Response contains the AIOps TEP Session Phase A opening header (e.g. 'AIOps Task, Estimation & Planning — Session 3 of 3' or equivalent)

assertions.name: four-phase-structure-shown
assertions.description: Response includes a table or list showing the four phases (assumptions, task inventory, sizing, estimation roll-up) and their output files (s3-01 through s3-03)

assertions.name: confirms-session-2-inputs
assertions.description: Response references the required Session 2 inputs (s2-02 UC selection, s2-03 design, s2-04 plan)

assertions.name: confirms-tshirt-conversion
assertions.description: Response presents or offers to confirm the T-shirt conversion table (XS=1-2, S=3-5, M=6-10, L=11-20, XL=21-40 person-days) and allows override

assertions.name: no-premature-estimates
assertions.description: Response does NOT produce person-day estimates before building the task inventory and assumptions

evals.prompt: The TEP session for Meridian Logistics is done. I have my three session 3 files ready: s3-01 through s3-03. Time to generate the TEP report.
evals.expected_output: Phase B session opening: asks about report format, client name, logo, date, confidentiality. Does NOT re-run Phase A or rebuild the task inventory.

assertions.name: phase-b-correctly-identified
assertions.description: Response correctly identifies this as Phase B (report generation) rather than Phase A (interactive TEP)

assertions.name: asks-report-format
assertions.description: Response asks about report format options — at minimum mentions Accenture HTML and at least one alternative

assertions.name: asks-client-and-date
assertions.description: Response asks for client name (exact spelling/case) and document date

assertions.name: no-phase-a-content
assertions.description: Response does NOT attempt to rebuild the task inventory, re-apply sizing, or ask for Session 2 inputs to redo estimation

assertions.name: mentions-input-files
assertions.description: Response references or lists the three s3 input files needed (s3-01 through s3-03), optionally noting s2-04 for release context

evals.prompt: We have the selected use cases, the platform design and the release plan for a retail banking client. Now we need a work breakdown with effort estimates. How should I run this?
evals.expected_output: Correctly infers Phase A from the signals (has Session 2 outputs, wants work breakdown + estimates). Opens with the structured four-phase TEP protocol and builds tasks before sizing.

assertions.name: infers-phase-a-from-context
assertions.description: Response correctly infers this is Phase A (the user has Session 2 outputs and wants estimates) without asking the user to clarify Phase A vs Phase B

assertions.name: session-structure-shown
assertions.description: Response presents the structured four-phase session layout with output files

assertions.name: tasks-before-sizing
assertions.description: Response sequences the work as task inventory first, then T-shirt sizing, then person-day roll-up — not jumping straight to a headline number

assertions.name: estimates-with-bounds
assertions.description: Response indicates estimates will carry low and high person-day bounds rather than single-point figures
